uk: 🕃 Свв. пророка Захарії та праведної Єлисавети, батьків чесного Івана Предтечі
Преподобної матері нашої Терези з Калькутти
saints.en: Sts. Prophet Zechariah & Righteous Elizabeth, Parents of the Honorable John the Forerunner. Ven. Teresa of Calcutta

rubrics:
Вечірня: катизма чергова. На «Господи, взиваю» 6 стихир прор.; Сл.: прор., І нині: богородичний-догмат 5-го гласу. Прокімен дня. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар прор., Сл., І нині: богород. воскресний за гласом тропаря прор. Відпуст великий з помин. цілого тижня і прор.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар прор. (2), Сл., І нині: богород. воскресний за гласом тропаря мчч. Катизми чергові, сідальні Октоїха. Пс. 50. Канон: якщо Господній або Богородичний храм – канонів 3: храму, прор. та Октоїха. Якщо храм святого, канонів 3: прор., храмового святого та Октоїха. По 9-ій пісні – світильний Октоїха, Сл.: прор., І нині: його богород. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар прор., Сл., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря прор. і за днем тижня. Відпуст великий з помин. усього тижня і прор.
Часи: на 1-му – тропар дня; на 6-му – храму; на 3-му та 9-му – прор.
Літургія: вся Служба – дня. Ап. – 1 Кор. 132 зач.; 4, 17 – 5, 5. Єв. – Мт. 97 зач.; 24, 1-13.

troparion_en.text: Robed in the vestments of the priesthood, / according to the Law of God you offered whole-burnt offerings in a sacred manner, wise Zachariah. / You became a luminary and a seer of the mysteries, / bearing within yourself the signs of grace, all-wise one. / Slain by the sword in the temple of God, O prophet of Christ, / intercede together with the Forerunner / that our souls may be saved.

kontakion_en.text: Today the prophet Zachariah, priest of the Most High / and parent of the Forerunner, / has prepared a banquet to his memory to nourish the faithful, / mixing the drink of righteousness. / Therefore we praise him as a divine initiate of the grace of God.

matched_oca_saint: Holy Prophet Zachariah and Righteous Elizabeth, parents of Saint John the Baptist

oca_saints.name: Holy Prophet Zachariah and Righteous Elizabeth, parents of Saint John the Baptist

troparion.text: Robed in the vestments of the priesthood, / according to the Law of God you offered whole-burnt offerings in a sacred manner, wise Zachariah. / You became a luminary and a seer of the mysteries, / bearing within yourself the signs of grace, all-wise one. / Slain by the sword in the temple of God, O prophet of Christ, / intercede together with the Forerunner / that our souls may be saved.

kontakion.text: Today the prophet Zachariah, priest of the Most High / and parent of the Forerunner, / has prepared a banquet to his memory to nourish the faithful, / mixing the drink of righteousness. / Therefore we praise him as a divine initiate of the grace of God.

troparion.text: Blessed are you, our Father Athanasios, /   for you lived by faith, standing vigilantly for the holy Orthodox Faith, /   always singing the Akathist Hymn to the Most Pure one with humility,  /  ordering everyone not to profane the holy Orthodox Faith, you testified to its truth, suffering even unto death.  /  Honoring your holiness, we cry out to you with boldness: /   "O Hieromartyr Athanasios our Father, you are our boast and adornment."

kontakion.text: By your life, Athanasios, you were revealed as an ornament of the monastic life and adornment of the martyrs;  /  and as a most radiant sun you shone forth on the Orthodox people.  /  Therefore, Christ has enriched you with the gift of working miracles,  / so that honoring your memory, we may call out to you:  /  "Remember your flock in your prayers, O Venerable one."

troparion.text: O Martyr of the Lord, Prince Gleb, you were glorified because of your faith,  /  you are the speedy helper of those who toil, and the hope of the sick;  /  for you heal the afflictions of those who come to you with faith. /  Therefore, with faith we pray:  /  "Deliver us, who honor your memory with love, from our misfortune."

kontakion.text: Today the land is appeased, having drunk your blood,   /  and the temple is sanctified by receiving your body, O Holy Martyr Prince Gleb.  /   For of your own will, you did not resist your passion in any way.   /  From your fragrant relics, you give sanctification to the faithful, and to the world great mercy.

oca_saints.name: Martyr Tathuil, and his sister, Bebaia

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Tathuil and Bebaia, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

Your holy martyr Sarbelus, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Sarbelus;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

oca_saints.name: Martyrs Juventinus and Maximus the soldiers, at Antioch

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Juventinus and Maximus, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Martyrs Urban, Theodore, Medimnus, and 77 companions, at Nicomedia

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

Your holy martyr Abdias, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Abdias;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.
